chunks.text:
Online safety regulatory documents and guidance
Online safety regulatory documents and guidance
Online safety
Illegal and harmful content
Protecting children
Information for industry
Published:
23 October 2025
This page provides the latest versions of Ofcom’s online safety regulatory documents and guidance, as well as links to previous, superseded versions. For further information about how to comply with the duties and the steps service providers need to take, visit our
guide for services page
.
We have published statements where you can also find summaries of our decisions and documents which explain the context and our reasoning.
Statements
Statement: Online Safety Transparency Reporting

- published 21 July 2025
Statement on Online Safety fees and penalties

chunks.text: need to take, visit our guide for services page . We have published statements where you can also find summaries of our decisions and documents which explain the context and our reasoning. Statements Statement: Online Safety Transparency Reporting - published 21 July 2025 Statement on Online Safety fees and penalties - published 26 June 2025 Statement: Protecting children from harms online - published 24 April 2025 Statement: Age Assurance and Children’s Access - published 16 January 2025 Statement: Protecting people from illegal harms online - published 16 December 2024 Illegal content risk assessments Ofcom’s Risk Assessment Guidance aims to help service providers comply with the illegal content duties and contains our ‘Risk Profiles’ that service providers must consult. Risk Assessment Guidance and Risk Profiles • PDF • 902.38 KB • 7 February 2025 Children access assessment and children's risk assessments Ofcom’s guidance on Children’s Access Assessments helps service providers comply with their duties on these assessments. Ofcom’s Children's Risk Assessment Guidance aims to help service providers comply with the children’s risk assessment duties and contains our ‘Risk Profiles’ that service providers must consult. Children's Access Assessments Guidance • PDF • 968.02 KB • 9 May 2025 Children’s Risk Assessment Guidance and Children’s Risk Profiles • PDF • 884.07 KB • 9 May 2025 Codes of Practice The Codes of Practice set out measures recommended by Ofcom for service providers to comply with their safety duties and other duties specified under the Act. Illegal content Codes of Practice for user-to-user services • PDF • 893.65 KB • 26 February 2025 Illegal content Codes of Practice for search services • PDF • 676.35 KB • 26 February 2025 Issued Protection of Children Code of Practice for user-to-user services • PDF • 947.98 KB • 4 July 2025 Issued Protection of Children Code of Practice for search services • PDF • 684.42 KB • 4 July 2025 Previous versions of Codes of Practice Illegal content DRAFT Illegal content Codes of Practice for search services (PDF, 660 KB)

chunks.text: user-to-user services • PDF • 947.98 KB • 4 July 2025 Issued Protection of Children Code of Practice for search services • PDF • 684.42 KB • 4 July 2025 Previous versions of Codes of Practice Illegal content DRAFT Illegal content Codes of Practice for search services (PDF, 660 KB) DRAFT Illegal content Codes of Practice for user-to-user services (PDF, 970.54 KB) Protection of Children DRAFT Protection of Children Code for search services (PDF, 660.02 KB) DRAFT Protection of Children Code for user-to-user services (PDF, 924.5 KB) Causes and impacts of harms The Registers of Risks are our assessments of the causes and impacts of illegal harms and content harmful to children online. Register of Risks • PDF • 5.69 MB • 7 February 2025 Children's Register of Risks • PDF • 3.66 MB • 30 July 2025 Content guidance These documents provide guidance on what constitutes illegal content and content harmful to children to support service providers making these judgements. Illegal Content Judgements Guidance (ICJG) (updated) • PDF • 2.76 MB • 20 August 2025 Guidance on content harmful to children • PDF • 802.06 KB • 24 April 2025 Previous version of content guidance Illegal Content Judgements Guidance (superseded) (PDF, 3.09 MB) Record-keeping and review duties Ofcom’s Record-Keeping and Review Guidance assists service providers with the requirements to keep records of their risk assessments and the measures taken to comply with certain online safety duties, and to review them regularly. Record-Keeping and Review Guidance • PDF • 261.31 KB • 24 April 2025 Other online safety guidance for service providers These documents provide guidance on highly effective age assurance, the concepts of content communicated ‘publicly’ and ‘privately’ within the Act and when and how Ofcom will exercise its transparency powers. Part 3 Guidance on highly effective age assurance • PDF • 394.54 KB • 24 April 2025 Guidance on highly effective age assurance and other Part 5 duties • PDF • 775.13 KB • 16 January 2025 Guidance on content communicated 'publicly' and 'privately' under the Online Safety Act • PDF • 274.73 KB • 19 December 2024 Final Transparency Guidance • PDF • 400.12 KB • 21 July 2025 Guidance on Ofcom’s use of its powers These documents provide guidance on how Ofcom will normally approach enforcement and what our online safety information gathering powers are. Online Safety Enforcement Guidance • PDF • 1.37 MB • 26 February 2025 Online Safety Information Gathering Guidance • PDF • 730.67 KB • 26 February 2025 Stay up-to-date with consultations and important dates To stay up-to-date with current Online Safety consultations and upcoming dates for compliance duties, visit the important dates for online safety compliance page .
